question: A call activity is modelled with the XML shown. What is the effect of setting `propagateAllParentVariables="false"` on this call activity?

options.text: Variables from higher scopes of the parent are no longer copied into the child; only variables created by the call activity's input mappings reach the child.
All parent variables, including those from higher scopes, are still copied to the child as local variables.
The child process is denied access to its own input mappings, even if they are defined.
Variables produced by the child process are not propagated back to the parent on completion.

a.text: Setting `propagateAllParentVariables` to `false` stops parent-scope variables from being copied into the child. Only the variables produced by the call activity's input mappings are available in the child instance.

b.text: That describes the default (`true`). Setting the attribute to `false` is exactly what disables that behaviour.

c.text: The attribute controls parent-to-child variable copying, not whether input mappings are honoured. Input mappings still run.

d.text: Child-to-parent propagation is controlled by `propagateAllChildVariables` and output mappings, not by `propagateAllParentVariables`.

explanation: `propagateAllParentVariables` controls the parent-to-child variable flow at a call activity. With it set to `false`, variables from higher scopes are not copied into the child; the child only receives the variables explicitly produced by the call activity's input mappings.

codeBlocks.stem:
<bpmn:callActivity id="Call_Activity" name="Call Process A">
  <bpmn:extensionElements>
    <zeebe:calledElement processId="child-process-id" propagateAllParentVariables="false" />
    <zeebe:ioMapping>
      <zeebe:input source="=variableValue" target="variableName" />
    </zeebe:ioMapping>
  </bpmn:extensionElements>
</bpmn:callActivity>
